look at the details
at 30 pulls you will pity a "highest rarity" ssr. The specific banner character guarantee is at 90. Average pulls to hit the banner character is around 42.

Banners fall under a category. The red underline is where their category usually is. All banners under the same category share the same pity. You can click Blue to check the banner details, drop tables, and your pull history.

Generally, all banners have a guaranteed SSR at 30 pity. For Character Event Awakenings, there is a 33% chance of getting the rate-up limited, and a 66% chance of getting a standard unit of the same realm.
If you lose to a standard unit twice in Character Event Awakenings, then your third SSR will be the rate-up limited.
This means that Character Event Awakenings have a hard pity of 90 to get the rate-up unit, with an average pity of 42.

Alright...so...regarding Ultra space, the stuff you throw in there depends on a stage.
For example, if I know there will be a tough boss, my usual ultra space looks like this: 2 plumes + 1 horla emotion (usually vuln) + GRamona's 2 cost and either Castor's 2 cost or Kath's Last Stand.
Explanation: you need some time to stack plumes, so having a few when you enter the boss arena helps a lot. Horla emotion is for situation if I get unlucky and won't get vuln application for my ultra burst turn. GRamona's 2 cost is for any missing card you might want. Castor's for weakness and Last Stand for more STR stacking, since Kath needs a lot of STR.
-----------------------------------
If a stage doesn't have a tough boss, it will be smth like:
Horla's emotion, Solarflare, GRamona's 2 cost, Last Stand if there are Death Resists or just for more STR stacking and the last one is usually Castor's 2 cost.
Standard WoD Awakening has the same rules as Character Event Awakening.

Quick question, I am new to the game and was confused about monster targeting mechanic. When there are more than one enemy on the battlefield your attacks will only target the oen in the front? You cannot choose?
Yes. If there is an annoying enemy in the back you want to deal with first you are forced to use AOE or to rely on random targeting abilities to hit them.
Ok that confirmed what I thought thanks. Is there are a reason for that?
yes
it's a deliberate design choice
Game is very much balanced around this targeting system.
battles are designed so that you have to interface with enemies
you can't just oneshot a squishy backline enemy and ignore mechanics
you have to find ways around the frontline tanks, whether it be aoe or defense or dots or higher bursts
Ok thanks for the explanation that makes more sense now 🙂

https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1384463374213189652/1384463374213189652

Damage amp is a teamwide stat. It is a multiplier, but only on the character base damage and it is applied early on in the damage formula.

Crit damage is a personal stat. It is a multiplier and is applied later in the damage formula. Outcome of this is that pretty much every carry wants crit damage more than damage amp, not that amp is bad. Carries with low crit rate will also want crit rate.
